steps.title: Find what the 'but' clause signals
steps.content: The passage warns that treating Larsen's characterization as unproblematic risks missing subtleties in her style. This means the blank must describe what the reasons seem like—something that makes them appear acceptable without deeper examination.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the correct choice
steps.content:
”Irrefutable” (undeniable) directly captures this: reasons that appear so solid and beyond question that scholars accept the characterization without reservation.

This uncritical acceptance is precisely what the “but” clause warns leads to overlooking Larsen's unique qualities.

The other options don't describe this kind of surface-level certainty that masks deeper complexity.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
